title: Merck — Oncology — briefing Q4-2025
html:
What Changed in Merck Oncology — Q4 2025

Generated: 2026-04-24 Quarter covered: October - December 2025 Data sources: Merck Q4 2025 / FY2025 earnings call (Feb 3, 2026), FDA actions, ZoomRx study deliverables Reading time: ~12 minutes

1. TL;DR (5 Bullets)

  1. Keytruda + QLEX hit $8.4B in Q4 2025, closing FY2025 at $31.7B (+7% YoY). Growth driven by earlier-stage uptake and sustained metastatic demand. KEYTRUDA QLEX contributed $35M in Q4 ($40M for full year) — in line with management expectations but a tiny fraction of total.
  2. QLEX adoption is embryonic — $35M in Q4 on a $8.4B base means <0.5% penetration after ~3 months on market. Management characterized this as expected, citing the temporary J-code (J9999) as a major adoption friction. Permanent J-code J9277 confirmed effective April 1, 2026.
  3. Merck guided FY2026 revenue of $64.2-$65.7B with Keytruda expected to continue growing but at a moderating pace. The guide explicitly reflects acquisition costs (Prometheus/Terns deals) as Merck diversifies ahead of the 2028 LOE.
  4. KN-689 listening sessions and HCP-Pt conversations are generating rich post-launch data — ZoomRx's November 2025 listening session and January 2026 weekly check-in show continued HCP learning curve. Surgeons are engaged but cautious.
  5. SubQ IO HCP-Patient Conversations study kicked off November 2025 — 7 conversations collected across melanoma, NSCLC, and TNBC. Early but the study is in field and generating data for the QLEX adoption narrative.

2. Data Events That Matter

FY2025 Full-Year Results: Keytruda's Growth Moderates

  • What happened: $31.7B full year Keytruda revenue, +7% YoY (vs. +18% in FY2024). Q4 at $8.4B, +5% YoY.
  • What it means: The growth deceleration is expected — Keytruda is approaching peak revenue before the 2028 LOE. The absolute numbers remain extraordinary ($31.7B is the highest annual revenue for any drug in history), but the growth rate signals maturation. Earlier-stage indications (KN-671, KN-689) are the incremental growth drivers; metastatic indications are approaching saturation.
  • What Vinoth should say: "7% growth on a $30B base is still adding $2B annually in absolute terms. But the growth rate curve matters for Merck's stock story. The question is whether KN-689 + QLEX + ovarian + urothelial expansions can maintain mid-single-digit growth through 2027, or whether growth stalls below 5%. Our tracking studies measure the leading indicators of that answer."

QLEX Q4: $35M Is the Starting Line, Not the Verdict

  • What happened: KEYTRUDA QLEX generated $35M in Q4 2025, its first full quarter on market.
  • What it means: On a $8.4B base, $35M is essentially a rounding error. But this was expected — the temporary J-code created billing uncertainty, physician inertia with new formulations is real, and site-of-care workflows needed adjustment. The real test begins Q2 2026 after the permanent J-code takes effect April 1.
  • What Vinoth should say: "Don't judge QLEX by Q4 numbers — there's a billing code problem, not a demand problem. The April 2026 J-code switch is the unlock. Our SubQ IO study will be the first primary research to show whether the J-code changes conversation dynamics."

Merck's Diversification Accelerates

  • What happened: FY2026 guidance of $64.2-$65.7B reflects Prometheus (immunology) and Terns (CML) acquisitions. Merck signaled $70B+ annual opportunity over the next decade from pipeline assets.
  • What it means: Merck is preparing for a post-Keytruda future. The investment thesis is shifting from "Keytruda growth" to "can Merck replace Keytruda revenue fast enough?" The oncology commercial infrastructure (field force, MSLs, medical affairs) will need to support both Keytruda and the emerging portfolio simultaneously.

ZoomRx Study Updates

  • KN-689 Final Report v4 (Pre-read): Fielded Aug 2025-Feb 2026. 7 HCP-Pt conversations + 14 HCP interviews. Rich post-launch data on PD-L1 testing evolution, MDT dynamics, and KN-689 experience. Key findings: positive HCP experience with KN-689, but PD-L1 testing infrastructure still maturing.
  • SubQ IO Kickoff (Nov 2025): 7 conversations collected (4 MD, 3 NP/PA) across melanoma, adjuvant NSCLC, metastatic NSCLC, and neoadjuvant TNBC. Study is ramping recruitment.
  • KN-689 Listening Sessions: November 2025 and January 2026 sessions shared with Merck team, providing ongoing qualitative signal.

3. Competitor Earnings Intelligence

Revenue Snapshot — Q4 2025

Company Brand Q4 2025 Revenue YoY Growth Key Signal
BMS Opdivo ~$2.5B +6% ~40 approved indications; perioperative NSCLC expansion building
AstraZeneca Imfinzi ~$1.2B +18% AEGEAN perioperative NSCLC building; fastest-growing IO competitor
AstraZeneca DATROWAY ~$50M N/A (ramp) Early ramp in HR+/HER2- mBC; ADC class momentum building
Roche Tecentriq ~$850M -10% Continued decline; Roche acknowledged IO competitive pressure
Keytruda $8.4B +5% Context: FY2025 $31.7B; growth moderating but absolute revenue still dominant

BMS — Opdivo (~$2.5B, +6%) + Opdualag (growing)

BMS Q4 2025 / FY2025 call (January 2026):

  1. Opdivo ended FY2025 at ~$9.8B, approaching $10B annual run rate: BMS highlighted this milestone as evidence of the franchise's durability. However, growth of 6% on a $10B base is adding only ~$600M annually — a fraction of Keytruda's ~$2B annual absolute growth.

  2. Perioperative NSCLC is BMS's most aggressive expansion play: BMS continued to emphasize CheckMate-77T data, positioning perioperative Opdivo as a competitive alternative to Keytruda's KN-671 in resectable NSCLC. Management noted that the perioperative IO market is "still forming" and claimed differentiated data in certain biomarker subgroups.

  3. BMS now explicitly managing toward biosimilar entry (2027-2028): Q4 commentary included more detailed lifecycle management discussion — subcutaneous Opdivo formulations in development, Opdualag expansion in melanoma subtypes, and next-generation combinations. BMS is running the Opdivo LOE playbook 1-2 years ahead of Merck's Keytruda LOE.

  4. Opdualag growth remains melanoma-confined: Despite positive Phase 3 data, Opdualag has not meaningfully expanded beyond melanoma. BMS is not positioning it as a broad IO combination platform — it's a melanoma lifecycle extension.

Implication for Merck: BMS's explicit LOE preparation commentary is a preview of the conversation Merck will have in 2027. The parallel is instructive: BMS is investing in SC formulation, niche combinations, and lifecycle extensions. Merck's QLEX strategy is more advanced (already approved), but BMS's combination approach (Opdualag) is a different defensive vector Merck lacks.

Roche — Tecentriq (~$850M, -10%)

Roche FY2025 call:

  1. Tecentriq declined 10% YoY in Q4, ending FY2025 at ~$3.5B: Roche acknowledged that Tecentriq faces "structural competitive pressure" from Keytruda and Opdivo, particularly in NSCLC and urothelial cancer — Tecentriq's two largest indications.

  2. Roche explicitly deprioritizing Tecentriq in promotional investment: Management signaled that incremental IO investment is going to combination strategies (Tecentriq + tiragolumab, Tecentriq + ADCs) rather than Tecentriq monotherapy or standard doublet expansion.

  3. SKYSCRAPER Phase 3 mixed results temper TIGIT enthusiasm: Roche's anti-TIGIT combination data was mixed, with one trial missing the primary endpoint. This weakens the Tecentriq lifecycle extension story and reinforces the view that Tecentriq is in managed decline.

Implication for Merck: Tecentriq's accelerating decline (-10% vs. -8% in Q3) confirms the IO market is consolidating around Keytruda and Opdivo. Roche is no longer a competitive threat in IO monotherapy. The strategic implication is that Keytruda's near-term competition comes from BMS (perioperative) and AZ (Stage III/SCLC), not from Roche.

AstraZeneca — Imfinzi ($1.2B, +18%) + DATROWAY ($50M)

AZ Q4 2025 / FY2025 call:

  1. Imfinzi ended FY2025 at ~$4.5B, +15% YoY — the fastest-growing IO franchise after Keytruda: AZ positioned Imfinzi as the #2 IO in lung cancer (behind Keytruda) and the clear leader in Stage III NSCLC (PACIFIC) and LS-SCLC (ADRIATIC). FY2025 was the first year Imfinzi exceeded Tecentriq's annual revenue.

  2. AEGEAN perioperative NSCLC data building, but Keytruda's KN-671 has the earlier approval: AZ acknowledged that perioperative IO competition is intensifying. AEGEAN (durvalumab + chemotherapy before/after surgery) showed strong EFS improvement, but Keytruda's KN-671 approval in July 2024 gave Merck first-mover advantage.

  3. DATROWAY at ~$50M in Q4 signals ADC traction in breast cancer: The ADC is ramping in HR+/HER2- metastatic breast cancer. While not directly competitive with Keytruda in the same tumors, DATROWAY represents the broader ADC + IO convergence trend.

  4. AZ specifically noted Keytruda's perioperative expansion as competitive pressure on Imfinzi's Stage III positioning: Management stated that as more patients receive perioperative IO (Keytruda), fewer reach unresectable Stage III — Imfinzi's core market. This is a structural headwind for Imfinzi.

Implication for Merck: Imfinzi's 18% growth makes it the most dynamic IO competitor. But AZ's own admission that Keytruda's perioperative expansion erodes Imfinzi's Stage III market validates Merck's TAM expansion thesis. The perioperative strategy isn't just adding patients — it's reducing competitors' addressable markets.

Synthesis: What the Combined Q4 2025 Picture Tells Us

  1. The IO market is consolidating into a two-player structure with a growing gap. Keytruda ($31.7B FY2025) leads Opdivo ($9.8B) by >3x. Imfinzi ($4.5B) is a distant third. Tecentriq ($3.5B) is declining. The rest are immaterial. Keytruda's dominance is structural, not cyclical.

  2. Perioperative IO is the competitive frontier, and Merck is winning. KN-671 (NSCLC) and KN-689 (HNSCC) give Keytruda first-mover advantage in the fastest-growing IO segment. BMS (CheckMate-77T) and AZ (AEGEAN) are countering, but approvals lag Merck's.

  3. BMS's LOE preparation playbook is instructive for Merck. BMS is 1-2 years ahead in LOE planning (biosimilar entry 2027-2028 vs. Keytruda 2028). BMS's approach — SC formulation, niche combinations, lifecycle extensions — mirrors Merck's QLEX strategy. BMS's experience will preview what works and what doesn't.

  4. The ADC class is the next-generation competitive vector. DATROWAY's ramp, Roche's pivot to ADCs/bispecifics, and the broader industry trend toward IO + ADC combinations signal that Keytruda's long-term defense requires being the IO backbone of ADC combinations — not just the standalone leader.

4. Commercial Pulse

Metric Keytruda (IV) Keytruda QLEX (SC) Combined
Q4 2025 Revenue ~$8.37B $35M $8.4B
YoY Growth +5% N/A +5%
FY2025 Revenue ~$31.66B $40M $31.7B
FY2025 YoY Growth +7% N/A +7%
QLEX Penetration — <0.5% —
QLEX FY Guidance — Not broken out —

Key commercial metric: QLEX's share of total Keytruda revenue. Must reach 10%+ by end of 2026 to be on track for the 30-40% target by 2028.

What Merck management said on the Q4 2025 / FY2025 call:

  1. "2026 is the year QLEX inflects — permanent J-code April 1 changes everything." Management was unambiguous: they see the temporary J-code as the primary adoption barrier and expect a step-change in Q2 2026 onward.
  2. On the LOE defense: "We are building a Keytruda franchise that survives LOE." This is a narrative upgrade from Q3's 3-prong defense — management is now framing post-LOE as a "franchise" outcome, not just a mitigation strategy.
  3. "30-40% SubQ conversion by 2028 protects meaningful revenue." This was the first specific quantified target for QLEX conversion. It implies management expects $9-12B of Keytruda's peak revenue to flow through QLEX by 2028.
  4. The ovarian cancer approval (February 2026) and perioperative bladder expansion were positioned as proof points that the TAM expansion strategy is on track. Management's guidance of $64.2-65.7B for FY2026 explicitly names Keytruda, QLEX, and perioperative expansion as the three growth priorities.

Key analyst Q&A signals:

  1. Analysts asked directly: "What's the realistic post-2028 floor?" Management gave the 30-40% SubQ target but did not commit to a specific revenue floor number — hedging on how much of the IV franchise survives biosimilar entry.
  2. QLEX at $35M generated skepticism. The $35M on a $8.4B base (<0.5% penetration) prompted questions about whether the 30-40% target is achievable in 3 years. Management pointed to Darzalex Faspro and Rituxan SC precedents but did not provide a specific adoption curve projection.
  3. FY2026 guidance missed expectations. Analysts noted that GARDASIL weakness + Keytruda growth moderation + LOE concerns were all weighing on the outlook. The guidance conversation was more defensive than aspirational.

5. Thesis Update

Thesis Status Key Evidence This Quarter
1. QLEX adoption determines post-2028 floor UNCHANGED $35M Q4 is embryonic. J-code friction confirmed. Real test = Q2 2026.
2. KN-689 slow burn — surgeons are bottleneck STRENGTHENED KN-689 Final Report confirms positive but cautious adoption. Community surgeons still lag. PD-L1 testing at 4/13 formalized.
3. Perioperative TAM expansion is right bet UNCHANGED FY2025 growth explicitly attributed to "earlier-stage cancers." Strategy confirmed.
4. MDT is the intervention point STRENGTHENED KN-689 Final Report v4 provides the most detailed MDT dynamics data to date. 20-40% leakage quantified.
5. Three-pronged LOE defense UNCHANGED $3B cost cuts proceeding. QLEX launched. Perioperative expansion continuing. All three legs active.

6. What to Watch Next Quarter (Q1 2026)

  1. QLEX J-code transition (April 1, 2026) — J9277 replaces J9999. This is the single most important adoption catalyst. If Q1 2026 QLEX revenue doesn't show step-change in the April+ portion, the adoption thesis weakens.
  2. Ovarian cancer approval — expected early 2026 (KEYNOTE-100/KEYLYNK data). Would add another new indication and reinforce the women's cancers growth vector.
  3. Merck Q1 2026 earnings (April 30, 2026) — first quarterly results that could show early J-code impact. Consensus estimate ~$7.7B for Keytruda.
  4. KN-689 ATU W2 — if fielded in Aug 2025 as planned, results should be available Q1 2026. First quantitative post-launch adoption measurement.
  5. SubQ IO study recruitment — needs 30 conversations + 10 follow-up quals. At 7 conversations after 2 months, pace needs to accelerate.

7. Conversation Starters for Merck

  1. "Your KN-689 final report shows exactly what we predicted: positive HCP experience but infrastructure gaps holding back adoption. The four leverage points we identified in the biomarker testing qual (FNA, reflexive protocols, surgeon ordering, MDT coverage) are the actionable playbook. Let us track these quarterly." — Converts research into an ongoing measurement engagement.

  2. "QLEX at $35M is the starting line. We've kicked off the SubQ IO conversation study and will have the first real-world conversation data by Q2 2026 — coinciding perfectly with the J-code transition. No one else has this data." — Creates urgency around the SubQ study's timing advantage.

Back to TA Knowledge Hub | Home
